parsed.summary: This cumulative diff (v0.4.0→v0.4.6) spans 6,600+ lines across 84 files, deleting one core module (process.mjs) and introducing two new critical-infrastructure modules (retry.mjs, bigmodel-errors.mjs). The retry/backoff and error-dispatch systems are the kind of change that looks correct on the happy path but hides class-A failure modes under concurrent load, partial outage, or API version skew. Without verifying that (a) no live import path still references the deleted process.mjs, (b) retries are gated on operation idempotency, and (c) bigmodel-errors.mjs does not silently swallow unmapped error codes, this should not ship to a sweep run.

findings.title: process.mjs deleted — latent MODULE_NOT_FOUND risk across import graph
findings.body: The diff stat shows `scripts/lib/process.mjs | 67 ---` (entire file removed). At least six other runtime files were modified in the same span (glm-companion.mjs, glm-client.mjs, render.mjs, state.mjs, tracked-jobs.mjs, job-control.mjs). The commit log's own Bundle F message ('ReferenceError + full thread/resume scaffolding purge') confirms prior breakage from this module's removal. If any surviving import references `process.mjs` — including dynamic imports, re-exports, or the hook script `session-lifecycle-hook.mjs` — the runtime will throw MODULE_NOT_FOUND at call time, not at startup, making it a silent-latent failure that passes smoke tests.
findings.recommendation: Run `grep -r "process" scripts/ --include='*.mjs' -l` and `node -e "import('./scripts/glm-companion.mjs')" ` against the built tree to confirm zero remaining references. If any exist, either restore the module or migrate the imports before the sweep.

findings.title: New retry.mjs (203 lines) may retry non-idempotent operations
findings.body: retry.mjs is an entirely new module (203 lines added, 0 prior) introduced as part of the 'BigModel error dispatch + auto retry/backoff' feature (v0.4.6 commit e0a9ba8). The companion modules glm-client.mjs (155 lines changed) and tracked-jobs.mjs (78 lines changed) suggest retries are applied to API calls that may mutate server-side state (job submission, status transitions). If a request reaches the server but the response is lost or returns a retriable error code, the automatic retry will issue a duplicate mutation. The 352-line test file is substantial but test scope cannot be verified from the stat alone — it may cover backoff timing without covering idempotency guards.
findings.recommendation: Verify that every callsite passing through retry.mjs either (a) uses an idempotency key / request-id header, or (b) is a read-only operation. Add a guard that refuses to retry mutating HTTP methods (POST, PUT, PATCH) unless an explicit `idempotent: true` flag is set by the caller. Document which operations are opted in.

findings.title: bigmodel-errors.mjs error classification may swallow unmapped error codes
findings.body: bigmodel-errors.mjs (168 lines, entirely new) implements error dispatch for BigModel (GLM) API responses. Error classification modules commonly use a catch-all/default category that either logs at a low level or returns a generic error, masking new or changed API error codes. Under API version skew (the sampling-sweep context suggests varied model versions), the server may return error codes not present in the classifier's map. If the default handler downgrades these to non-retriable or silently returns, jobs will fail without actionable diagnostics — exactly the kind of observability gap that is expensive to detect in a sweep.
findings.recommendation: Ensure the default/unknown error path (1) logs the raw error code and full response body at WARN or ERROR level, (2) surfaces the unmapped code in the thrown/rejected error so downstream callers and the sweep harness can detect it, and (3) is covered by a unit test that passes an unexpected code and asserts it is not silently consumed.

findings.title: glm-client.mjs heavily modified alongside retry introduction — stale error-path assumptions
findings.body: glm-client.mjs shows 155 lines changed in the same commit that introduced retry.mjs and bigmodel-errors.mjs. This is the core API client. The simultaneous modification of error handling (bigmodel-errors), retry policy (retry), and the client itself creates a three-way coupling where a mismatch in assumptions (e.g., client throws a new error shape that retry.mjs does not recognize, or bigmodel-errors classifies a timeout as non-retriable when the client expects a retry) will produce silently wrong behavior. The risk is amplified in a cumulative sweep across v0.4.x versions where different GLM model endpoints may have different error behaviors.
findings.recommendation: Add an integration-level test that simulates a complete request cycle: client call → server error → bigmodel-errors classification → retry decision → final outcome. Verify that every error code the client can produce is handled by the retry layer. Consider a contract test that asserts the error shapes produced by glm-client are a subset of what bigmodel-errors and retry.mjs expect.

next_steps: Run a full import graph check for any remaining references to the deleted process.mjs
Audit every callsite that feeds into retry.mjs to confirm idempotency or add request-deduplication keys
Verify the default/unknown error branch in bigmodel-errors.mjs is noisy enough to surface in sweep logs
Add at least one integration test covering the client→error-classify→retry pipeline end-to-end before the C3-v04x-cumulative sweep
